title: Add JSON schema for Helm chart values
description:
# GitHub Issue #189: Add JSON schema for Helm chart values

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Create a values.schema.json file for the Helm chart that validates all values.yaml fields with proper types, constraints, descriptions, and defaults. Include constraints like minimum replica counts, valid log levels, valid image pull policies, port ranges, and resource request/limit formats. Reference it from Chart.yaml.

**Motivation**: Without a JSON schema, users can set invalid values (e.g., replicas: -1, logLevel: 'verbose') without any feedback until Helm template rendering or runtime failure. A schema provides IDE autocompletion, early validation during helm install/upgrade, and serves as living documentation of all configuration options.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/charts/keystone-operator/`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] values.schema.json covers all fields in values.yaml
- [ ] Schema enforces type constraints, enums for known value sets, and min/max where applicable
- [ ] helm install with invalid values produces a clear validation error
- [ ] IDE autocompletion works with the schema in VS Code with the Helm extension
- [ ] Schema is referenced in Chart.yaml

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/189

stories.title: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
stories.want: Helm to reject invalid values at install/upgrade time before any templates are rendered
stories.so_that: I catch configuration errors immediately rather than debugging runtime failures
criteria: helm install with replicas: -1 fails with a clear validation error mentioning minimum value
helm install with pullPolicy: 'Sometimes' fails with a clear error listing valid options
helm install with metrics.port: 99999 fails with a clear error about port range
helm install with valid default values succeeds without any schema validation errors

stories.title: Chart developer gets IDE autocompletion for values
stories.want: my IDE to provide autocompletion and inline documentation for all values.yaml fields
stories.so_that: I can discover available configuration options without reading source templates
criteria: values.schema.json is a valid JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12 or Draft-07)
Every property has a 'description' field explaining its purpose
Default values in the schema match the defaults in values.yaml
Schema is referenced from Chart.yaml so tooling discovers it automatically

stories.title: Operator deployer gets validation of type constraints
stories.want: the schema to enforce correct types for all values fields
stories.so_that: type mismatches (e.g., string where integer expected) are caught before deployment
criteria: replicas rejects non-integer values (e.g., replicas: 'two')
Boolean fields (webhook.enabled, leaderElection.enabled, rbac.namespaceScoped, serviceAccount.create) reject non-boolean values
metrics.port rejects non-integer values
image.repository and image.tag accept only strings

stories.title: Operator deployer provides valid resource requests and limits
stories.want: the schema to accept valid Kubernetes resource quantity formats for CPU and memory
stories.so_that: I can use standard Kubernetes resource notation (e.g., 500m, 128Mi) without false rejections
criteria: resources.limits.cpu accepts valid patterns like '500m', '1', '0.5', '100m'
resources.limits.memory accepts valid patterns like '128Mi', '1Gi', '256M'
resources.requests.cpu and resources.requests.memory follow the same validation
Schema uses pattern matching for Kubernetes resource quantity format

stories.title: CI pipeline validates chart with schema
stories.want: helm lint to automatically validate the schema is well-formed and consistent with values.yaml
stories.so_that: schema drift from values.yaml is caught in the existing CI helm-validate job
criteria: helm lint passes with the schema present in the chart directory
Existing helm template scenarios in CI continue to pass with schema validation
helm unittest continues to pass with schema present
No changes needed to CI workflow since helm lint already validates schema when present

requirements.description: The chart SHALL include a values.schema.json file that covers every top-level and nested field defined in values.yaml
requirements.rationale: Ensures all configuration options are validated, preventing partial coverage that gives false confidence

scenarios.name: All values.yaml fields present in schema
scenarios.when: the schema is compared against values.yaml
scenarios.then: every field in values.yaml has a corresponding property definition in the schema
and_then: no values.yaml field is missing from the schema

scenarios.name: Schema validates default values
scenarios.when: helm lint is run with no value overrides (using default values.yaml)
scenarios.then: no schema validation errors are reported

scenarios.name: Additional properties are rejected at root level
scenarios.when: a user sets an undefined field (e.g., --set foo=bar)
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a validation error indicating the unexpected property
and_then: the error message identifies the unknown property name

requirements.description: The schema SHALL enforce correct JSON Schema types for all properties (integer, boolean, string, object)
requirements.rationale: Type enforcement prevents the most common class of misconfiguration errors

scenarios.name: Integer fields reject strings
scenarios.when: replicas is set to a string value like 'two'
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a type validation error
and_then: the error identifies the expected type as integer

scenarios.name: Boolean fields reject strings
scenarios.when: webhook.enabled is set to 'yes' (a string, not boolean)
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a type validation error
and_then: the error identifies the expected type as boolean

scenarios.name: String fields accept strings
scenarios.when: image.repository is set to a valid registry path string
scenarios.then: schema validation passes

requirements.description: The schema SHALL enforce enum constraints for fields with a known set of valid values
requirements.rationale: Enum validation catches typos and invalid options that would silently pass without schema

scenarios.name: Valid pullPolicy accepted
scenarios.when: image.pullPolicy is set to 'Always', 'IfNotPresent', or 'Never'
scenarios.then: schema validation passes

scenarios.name: Invalid pullPolicy rejected
scenarios.when: image.pullPolicy is set to 'Sometimes' or any other non-enum value
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a validation error listing allowed values
and_then: the error message includes the enum options

requirements.description: The schema SHALL enforce minimum/maximum constraints where semantically appropriate
requirements.rationale: Range validation prevents nonsensical values like negative replicas or out-of-range ports

scenarios.name: Replicas minimum enforced
scenarios.when: replicas is set to 0 or a negative number
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a validation error about minimum value
and_then: the error indicates the minimum allowed value is 1

scenarios.name: Metrics port range enforced
scenarios.when: metrics.port is set to 0 or 65536
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a validation error about port range
and_then: the error indicates the valid range (1-65535)

scenarios.name: Valid replicas accepted
scenarios.when: replicas is set to 1 or higher
scenarios.then: schema validation passes

requirements.description: The schema SHALL include a description field for every property to serve as living documentation
requirements.rationale: Descriptions enable IDE autocompletion tooltips and serve as documentation for users unfamiliar with the chart

scenarios.name: All properties have descriptions
scenarios.when: the schema is inspected programmatically
scenarios.then: every property definition includes a non-empty 'description' field
and_then: descriptions are clear and mention constraints where applicable

scenarios.name: Descriptions match values.yaml comments
scenarios.when: descriptions are compared against values.yaml inline comments
scenarios.then: the schema descriptions are consistent with or more detailed than the YAML comments

requirements.description: The schema SHALL specify default values that match values.yaml defaults
requirements.rationale: Matching defaults ensure IDE tooling shows the actual default values, preventing confusion

scenarios.name: Schema defaults match values.yaml
scenarios.when: each schema property with a 'default' is compared against values.yaml
scenarios.then: all default values match exactly

scenarios.name: Empty-string defaults represented correctly
scenarios.when: image.tag has an empty string default in values.yaml
scenarios.then: the schema also specifies default: '' for image.tag

requirements.description: Chart.yaml SHALL reference the schema file so Helm and tooling discover it automatically
requirements.rationale: Helm automatically loads values.schema.json from the chart root — no Chart.yaml reference needed for validation, but a comment serves as explicit documentation of the schema's presence

scenarios.name: Schema file placed at chart root
scenarios.when: helm lint is run on the chart
scenarios.then: Helm discovers and validates values.schema.json from the chart directory

scenarios.name: Chart.yaml version preserved
scenarios.when: Chart.yaml is modified
scenarios.then: apiVersion, name, version, and appVersion fields remain unchanged

requirements.description: The schema SHALL use additionalProperties: false at each object level to reject unknown fields
requirements.rationale: Rejecting unknown fields catches typos in field names that would otherwise be silently ignored

scenarios.name: Typo in top-level field caught
scenarios.when: a user sets --set replcia=3 (typo of replicas)
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a validation error about the unknown property

scenarios.name: Typo in nested field caught
scenarios.when: a user sets --set image.repo=foo (should be image.repository)
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a validation error about the unknown property under image

scenarios.name: Valid fields pass
scenarios.when: all fields match the schema property names exactly
scenarios.then: schema validation passes

requirements.description: The schema SHALL validate Kubernetes resource quantity strings with an appropriate pattern
requirements.rationale: Resource quantities have a specific format; validating the pattern prevents deployment failures from malformed resource specifications

scenarios.name: Valid CPU quantities accepted
scenarios.when: resources.limits.cpu is set to '500m', '1', '0.5', '2000m'
scenarios.then: schema validation passes for all valid patterns

scenarios.name: Valid memory quantities accepted
scenarios.when: resources.limits.memory is set to '128Mi', '1Gi', '256M', '512Ki'
scenarios.then: schema validation passes for all valid patterns

scenarios.name: Invalid resource quantity rejected
scenarios.when: resources.limits.cpu is set to 'lots' or '-1'
scenarios.then: helm install fails with a pattern validation error

requirements.description: Existing CI helm-validate job SHALL continue to pass with the schema in place
requirements.rationale: The schema must not break existing CI scenarios (helm lint, helm template, helm unittest)

scenarios.name: Default values pass schema
scenarios.when: helm lint is run with default values
scenarios.then: lint passes with no schema errors

scenarios.name: CI template scenarios pass schema
scenarios.when: all 5 existing helm template scenarios in CI are run
scenarios.then: all scenarios pass schema validation
and_then: webhook disabled, external SA, custom resources, namespace-scoped all pass

scenarios.name: Unittest suite passes
scenarios.when: helm unittest is run with the schema present
scenarios.then: all existing tests pass unchanged

tasks.title: Create values.schema.json with complete property definitions for all values.yaml fields (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-005, REQ-006)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.schema.json with JSON Schema Draft-07. Define every property from values.yaml: image (repository, tag, pullPolicy), replicas, resources (limits/requests with cpu/memory), rbac (namespaceScoped), leaderElection (enabled), webhook (enabled), metrics (port), serviceAccount (create, name). Also include nameOverride and fullnameOverride used in _helpers.tpl. Each property must have: type, description, default matching values.yaml. Use additionalProperties: false at all object levels.

tasks.title: Add enum constraints for image.pullPolicy (REQ-003)
tasks.description: In the values.schema.json created in 1.1, ensure image.pullPolicy has enum: ['Always', 'IfNotPresent', 'Never']. This is part of 1.1 but called out explicitly for traceability.

tasks.title: Add minimum/maximum constraints for replicas and metrics.port (REQ-004)
tasks.description: In the values.schema.json: replicas has minimum: 1. metrics.port has minimum: 1 and maximum: 65535. This is part of 1.1 but called out explicitly for traceability.

tasks.title: Add Kubernetes resource quantity pattern validation for resources fields (REQ-009)
tasks.description: In the values.schema.json: define a pattern for CPU and memory resource quantity strings. CPU pattern should accept: integer, decimal, millicpu (e.g., '500m', '1', '0.5'). Memory pattern should accept: plain integers and suffixed values (e.g., '128Mi', '1Gi', '256M', '512Ki'). Use regex patterns compatible with JSON Schema.

tasks.title: Add additionalProperties: false at all object levels (REQ-008)
tasks.description: Ensure every object in the schema (root, image, resources, resources.limits, resources.requests, rbac, leaderElection, webhook, metrics, serviceAccount) has additionalProperties: false. This is part of 1.1 but called out explicitly for traceability.

tasks.title: Update Chart.yaml to reference the schema (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Helm auto-discovers values.schema.json from the chart root directory. No Chart.yaml field is needed for discovery. However, the issue requests referencing it from Chart.yaml. Add a comment in Chart.yaml noting the schema file exists, or if Helm supports a 'schema' annotation, use that. Keep existing Chart.yaml fields unchanged.

tasks.title: Add helm-unittest tests for schema validation of invalid values (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-008, REQ-009) [unit]
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/values_schema_test.yaml. Test cases: (1) default values pass validation (helm template succeeds), (2) invalid pullPolicy value fails, (3) replicas below minimum fails, (4) metrics.port out of range fails, (5) unknown top-level property fails, (6) unknown nested property fails. Note: helm-unittest may not directly test schema validation — if not, create a shell-based test script that uses helm template with --set to validate. Verify approach against helm-unittest capabilities first.

tasks.title: Verify all existing CI helm template scenarios pass with schema (REQ-010) [integration]
tasks.description: Run locally all 5 CI helm template scenarios from .github/workflows/ci.yaml: (1) default values, (2) webhook.enabled=false, (3) serviceAccount.create=false + serviceAccount.name=existing-sa, (4) custom resources, (5) rbac.namespaceScoped=true + webhook.enabled=false. Also run helm lint and helm unittest. Fix any schema validation failures by adjusting the schema (not the CI scenarios).

tasks.title: Write Reference documentation for the values.schema.json (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Create docs/reference/backend/keystone-operator-helm-values-schema.md. Document: purpose of the schema, list of all validated fields with types/constraints/defaults in a table, how to use with IDE (VS Code Helm extension), how Helm validates values at install/upgrade time. Reference the existing docs/reference/backend/keystone-operator-packaging.md for chart packaging context.

test_specifications.test_function: should render successfully with default values
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with default values.yaml should succeed without schema errors [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should fail when an unknown top-level property is set
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set unknownField=true should fail with additionalProperties error [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should fail when image.pullPolicy is invalid
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set image.pullPolicy=Sometimes should fail with enum error [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should fail when replicas is below minimum
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set replicas=0 should fail with minimum value error [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should fail when replicas is negative
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set replicas=-1 should fail with minimum value error [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should fail when metrics.port is out of range (0)
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set metrics.port=0 should fail with minimum value error [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set metrics.port=70000 should fail with maximum value error [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should fail when unknown nested property is set under image
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer gets early validation of Helm values
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set image.repo=foo should fail with additionalProperties error [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should render successfully with webhook disabled
test_specifications.story: CI pipeline validates chart with schema
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set webhook.enabled=false should pass schema validation [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should render successfully with external service account
test_specifications.story: CI pipeline validates chart with schema
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set serviceAccount.create=false --set serviceAccount.name=existing-sa should pass [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should render successfully with namespace-scoped RBAC
test_specifications.story: CI pipeline validates chart with schema
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set rbac.namespaceScoped=true --set webhook.enabled=false should pass [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should render successfully with custom resource limits
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer provides valid resource requests and limits
test_specifications.expected: helm template with --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=64Mi should pass [unit]

test_specifications.test_function: should accept valid resource quantity patterns
test_specifications.story: Operator deployer provides valid resource requests and limits

test_specifications.test_function: should have descriptions for all properties
test_specifications.story: Chart developer gets IDE autocompletion for values
test_specifications.expected: Every property in the schema has a non-empty description field [unit]

test_specifications.story: Chart developer gets IDE autocompletion for values
test_specifications.expected: All schema defaults match the corresponding values in values.yaml [unit]

review_criteria: values.schema.json is valid JSON Schema (parseable, no syntax errors)
Every field in values.yaml has a corresponding property in the schema with correct type, description, and default
nameOverride and fullnameOverride from _helpers.tpl are included as optional string properties
additionalProperties: false is set on every object (root, image, resources, resources.limits, resources.requests, rbac, leaderElection, webhook, metrics, serviceAccount)
Enum constraint on image.pullPolicy matches Kubernetes spec: Always, IfNotPresent, Never
Resource quantity patterns accept standard K8s formats (500m, 128Mi, 1Gi, 0.5)
helm lint passes with default values and schema present
All 5 CI helm template scenarios pass with schema present
helm unittest passes with all existing tests unchanged
Schema defaults match values.yaml defaults exactly
Reference documentation covers all fields with types, constraints, and descriptions
